page_content: BSCI124 Plant Biology for Non-Science Students (3 Credits) A basic course in plant biology speciﬁcally designed for the non-science student. Emphasis is placed on an evolutionary and ecological approach to studying fundamental concepts and processes of plants, their place in the biosphere, the importance of plants to man, and the manner in which humans impact on plants and their environment. This course will not count toward graduation requirements for any student in Biological Sciences, Chemistry, Biochemistry or the College of Agriculture and Natural Resources. Restriction: For non-science majors only; and must not have completed BSCI105; and must not have completed BSCI170 or BSCI171. Additional Information: CORE Life Sciences Lab (LL) Course only when taken concurrently with BSCI125.
